_help: Edit this file to change the quiz. 'correct' is the 1-based index of the right answer (1 = first option, 2 = second, 3 = third, 4 = fourth). 'options' can hold any number of choices (2 or more). Lines starting with an underscore are ignored by the quiz.
title: Revolution & Collapse
subtitle: Russia and the First World War
intro: A quiz on Russia's collapse during the First World War, from the strain of the Eastern Front to the revolution that pulled it out of the fighting. Choose an answer to see what happened and why.

resultTiers.title: Full marks
resultTiers.blurb: Every answer correct. You know this story inside out.

resultTiers.title: Strong result
resultTiers.blurb: A firm grasp of how Russia fell out of the war.

resultTiers.title: Solid effort
resultTiers.blurb: A good foundation, with a few details worth revisiting.

resultTiers.title: Worth a review
resultTiers.blurb: Go back over the revolution and what it set in motion.

resultTiers.title: Time to revisit
resultTiers.blurb: Watch the documentary again and give it another go.

questions.question: Why was Russia exhausted by 1917?
options: A volcanic eruption destroyed their farmland
Russia had lost a war against Japan
Three years of war had devastated the country
Russia lost a lot of land
questions.explanation: By 1917 Russia had been at war for nearly three years, and the strain had worn the country down.

questions.question: What did peasants in Russia face in WWI?
questions.explanation: For ordinary Russians the war meant hunger and deepening poverty at home.

questions.question: Why had Tsar Nicholas lost the trust of his people?
options: He had converted to a different religion
He was seen as weak and unable to solve problems
He made peace with Germany too early
questions.explanation: Nicholas II came to be seen as weak and incapable of fixing the country's problems, and support for him drained away.

questions.question: Where did the February Revolution protests begin?
questions.explanation: The protests began in the capital, Petrograd (today's St Petersburg).

questions.question: What did the soldiers do during the February Revolution?
options: They refused to fire and joined the crowds
They fired on the protesting people
questions.explanation: Ordered to put down the protests, the soldiers refused to fire and joined the crowds instead — the turning point of the revolution.

questions.question: What does "abdicate" mean?
options: To move to a new country
questions.explanation: To abdicate means to give up the throne, which is what Nicholas II did.

questions.question: Which fatal mistake did the Provisional Government make?
options: It chose to continue the war
It gave land to the peasants
questions.explanation: The Provisional Government chose to keep fighting, and the hardship of the war kept public anger alive.

questions.question: What was the main slogan of the Bolsheviks?
questions.explanation: The Bolshevik slogan was 'Peace, Land, and Bread' — a direct answer to what people wanted most.

questions.question: How many people followed Vladimir Lenin?
questions.explanation: By this point around 200,000 people had rallied to Lenin's side.

questions.question: What was the main reason the Bolsheviks gained so much support?
options: They promised to make Russia stronger
They supported the continuation of war
They signed a treaty with France
They offered exactly what people needed
questions.explanation: The Bolsheviks won support by offering people exactly what they wanted, while the other parties did not.

questions.question: When exactly did Lenin return to Russia, and where had he been before?
questions.explanation: Lenin returned to Russia in April 1917 after years of exile abroad.

questions.question: What was the name of the peace treaty for Russia?
options: The Treaty of the Eastern Front
questions.explanation: Russia left the war by signing the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk with Germany in 1918.

questions.question: What long-term consequence of the Russian Revolution is mentioned in the conclusion?
options: Long-term tension between Russia and the Eastern Front
The rise of the Soviet Union
questions.explanation: The revolution's great long-term consequence was the rise of the Soviet Union, the world's first communist state.

questions.question: What does signing an armistice mean?
options: Two sides agree to stop fighting against each other
It means giving up a battle
questions.explanation: An armistice is an agreement between two sides to stop fighting.
